title: Swarm SOC feeds allow only one writer; collaboration uses N single-writer feeds
content:
# Single Writer Per Feed

Swarm feeds are backed by Single Owner Chunks (SOCs). A SOC update requires a signature from the owner's private key, so each feed has exactly one writer. This is a protocol-level constraint, not a Powerhouse choice.

## Implications

1. There is no "shared writable feed" — you cannot have Alice and Bob both pushing to one topic
2. Collaboration must be modeled as N writer feeds, one per participant
3. The collaboration index feed (itself single-writer by the creator) tells everyone where to subscribe
4. Each reader fans out across N-1 peer feeds to observe all edits

## Design consequence

The single-writer rule is why the op-feed layout uses `ph:v2:ops:<address>:<docId>` — address is part of the topic so each participant owns a distinct feed space for the same document. Without this, two participants would collide on the same topic and only one could actually write.
description: Swarm SOC feeds require the owner's private key to write; no feed is shared-writable. Collaboration composes N single-writer feeds rather than one shared feed.
